results.case_name: Agent NotReporting + App Degraded + Normal CPU
results.diagnosis: Azure VM agent and monitor agent are not reporting, which may be causing the degraded application health status.

results.case_name: Agent Failed + App Unhealthy + Memory Normal
results.diagnosis: Azure VM agent and monitor agent failures are likely causing the degraded resource health status and unhealthy application health status.

results.case_name: Agent Degraded + App Healthy + High Disk Latency
results.diagnosis: Azure VM agent and monitor agent are degraded, indicating potential issues with guest-level monitoring and management.

results.case_name: Agent Healthy + Monitor NotReporting + App Degraded
results.diagnosis: Application health is degraded, but VM and resource health appear normal.

results.case_name: Agent NotReporting + App Unhealthy + SSL Expiring
results.diagnosis: Azure VM is degraded and unhealthy due to potential communication issues with Azure services.

results.case_name: No Heartbeat + All Metrics Normal + Available
results.diagnosis: Azure VM is running but not sending heartbeats, indicating possible communication issues.

results.case_name: No Heartbeat + Moderate CPU + Degraded Health
results.diagnosis: The Azure VM is experiencing a degraded resource health status, likely due to a lack of heartbeat signal despite normal boot diagnostics and healthy agent statuses.

results.case_name: No Heartbeat + High Memory + Normal CPU
results.diagnosis: Azure VM Agent and Monitor Agent are degraded, indicating potential communication issues with Azure services.

results.case_name: No Heartbeat + Disk Pressure + App OK
results.diagnosis: The Azure VM is running but not sending heartbeats, indicating a potential issue with the monitoring or communication between the VM and Azure.

results.case_name: No Heartbeat + NSG Inconclusive + Normal
results.diagnosis: The Azure VM is running but heartbeat is not present, indicating possible communication issues.

results.case_name: Network Timeout + Healthy VM + RDP Allow
results.diagnosis: RDP connection timeout likely due to application or OS configuration issue.

results.case_name: SSH Timeout + RDP Allow + App Degraded
results.diagnosis: The Azure VM is experiencing application health issues despite normal system health metrics.

results.case_name: Both Timeout + NSG Allow + Healthy Agent
results.diagnosis: The Azure VM is experiencing connectivity issues with RDP and SSH timeouts, despite having healthy agent and monitor statuses.

results.case_name: Inconclusive Troubleshoot + High Memory
results.diagnosis: High memory usage may be impacting performance.

results.case_name: NSG Deny SSH + Troubleshoot Allow + App Unhealthy
results.diagnosis: Application health issue despite healthy Azure VM and network connectivity.

results.case_name: Degraded Health + Normal Metrics + No Annotation
results.diagnosis: Azure VM is running but has a degraded resource health status, indicating potential issues with underlying platform resources.

results.case_name: Unavailable Health + Running + Normal CPU
Degraded Health + Backup Failed + Moderate Load
results.diagnosis: Azure VM is experiencing degraded resource health despite normal boot diagnostics and healthy agent statuses.

results.case_name: Degraded Health + SSL Expired + Agent OK
results.diagnosis: SSL certificate has expired, which may be causing the degraded resource health status.

results.case_name: Unavailable Health + High Disk + App Degraded
results.diagnosis: Azure VM is experiencing degraded application health despite normal boot diagnostics and healthy agent status.

results.case_name: Moderate CPU + High Memory + Disk Pressure + App OK
results.diagnosis: High CPU and memory usage, but overall system health appears normal.

results.case_name: Low CPU + High Memory + Agent Degraded + Backup Failed
results.diagnosis: Azure VM agent and monitor agent are degraded, indicating potential issues with guest-level monitoring and management.

results.case_name: High CPU + Normal Memory + SSL Expiring + App Degraded
results.diagnosis: High CPU usage is likely causing the degraded resource health status and application health status.

results.case_name: Boot Stuck + Agent OK + App Healthy
results.diagnosis: Boot diagnostics are stuck, but the VM is otherwise healthy and responsive.

results.case_name: Normal Metrics + Disk IO High + Monitor Failed
results.diagnosis: Monitor agent failure may be causing issues, but overall VM health appears normal.

results.case_name: Platform Available + App Unhealthy + All Metrics Normal
results.diagnosis: Application health issue due to connection pool exhaustion.

results.case_name: Backup Failed + Disk 75% + SSL 7 days + App OK
results.diagnosis: Azure VM is running but last backup failed, which may indicate a potential issue with backup configuration or connectivity.

results.case_name: Memory 90% + CPU Normal + Agent Degraded + Disk OK
results.diagnosis: Azure VM Agent and Monitor Agent are degraded, indicating potential issues with guest-level monitoring and management.

results.case_name: CPU 80% + Disk 70% + Memory 70% + All Agents OK
results.diagnosis: The Azure VM is running with high CPU and memory utilization, but all other signals indicate normal operation.

results.case_name: Provisioning Updating + Running + Agent OK + App Degraded
results.diagnosis: The Azure VM is experiencing degraded resource health and application health, but system metrics are within normal ranges.
